name: Gradio
tagline: Build and share web demos for ML models in pure Python
seoTitle: Gradio - Build ML Web Apps in Python
metaDescription: Gradio is an open-source Python library for building shareable web UIs for machine learning models, APIs, and any function. No JavaScript or CSS needed.
overview: Gradio is an open-source Python package that lets you build a demo or web app for a machine learning model, an API, or any Python function. It takes only a few lines of code, and you don't need any JavaScript, CSS, or web hosting experience.

It's aimed at ML engineers, researchers, and developers who want to put a UI in front of a function so others can try it. You wrap your function with a Gradio interface, pick input and output components, and Gradio renders the full web page for you.

As a data app builder, Gradio focuses on quick, model-facing demos. It ships more than 30 built-in components for common ML inputs and outputs (text, images, audio, and more) and includes a built-in sharing feature that creates a public link for your running app.
features: Wrap any Python function in a web UI with the gr.Interface class
Over 30 built-in components for inputs and outputs, including Textbox, Image, and HTML
One-line public sharing: set share=True in launch() to get a public URL
Runs anywhere you write Python: code editor, Jupyter notebook, or Google Colab
Hot reload mode: run with the gradio command instead of python to auto-reload on file changes
No JavaScript, CSS, or web hosting experience required

gettingStarted.intro: Install Gradio with pip, then write a short Python script that wraps a function in an Interface and launches it.

steps.heading: Install Gradio
steps.body: Gradio requires Python 3.10 or higher. Installing inside a virtual environment is recommended.

steps.heading: Write your first app
steps.body: Create a file named app.py. Wrap a function with gr.Interface, choosing input and output components, then call launch().

steps.heading: Run it
steps.body: Run the file with Python. The demo opens in your browser at http://localhost:7860. Use the gradio command instead for hot reload mode.

steps.heading: Share a public link
steps.body: Set share=True in launch() to generate a publicly accessible URL for your running demo, with no web server setup.

useCases: Put an interactive demo in front of a machine learning model so non-developers can try it in a browser
Share a quick prototype with collaborators or reviewers using a temporary public link
Build a simple UI for an API or any Python function without writing frontend code
Demo a model inside a Jupyter notebook or Google Colab for teaching or experimentation
